description: Attijariwafa Bank is a Moroccan multinational banking and financial services company established through the merger of Wafabank and Banque Commerciale du Maroc. It operates in 27 countries across Africa and Europe, offering banking, asset management, insurance, and wealth management services.

Managem Group is a Moroccan multinational mining company established in 1930, specializing in the extraction, processing, and marketing of precious metals (gold, silver), base metals (copper, zinc, lead), cobalt, and fluorite. It operates across Morocco and six other African countries including Senegal, DRC, Sudan, Guinea, and Gabon. In 2025, Managem posted record revenue of MAD 13.7 billion driven by surging gold prices and new mine start-ups. It is controlled by the royal family holding company Al Mada.

Itissalat Al-Maghrib (Maroc Telecom) is a leading telecommunications provider offering mobile, fixed-line, and internet services, including 4G+ mobile internet, optical fiber, cloud hosting, and digital solutions across Morocco and internationally.
stock_exchange: Casablanca Stock Exchange (CSE: IAM) / Euronext Paris (IAM)

description: Marsa Maroc operates port terminals and manages logistics services across multiple ports in Morocco, focusing on international quality standards and efficient maritime operations.

Banque Centrale Populaire (BCP) is a Morocco-based banking and financial services group established in 1961. It operates as a credit institution and provides banking, insurance, and investment management services to individuals and businesses. It is the central body of the Crédit Populaire du Maroc network.

Bank of Africa is a Moroccan bank headquartered in Casablanca, providing financial services including banking, asset management, and insurance. It operates both domestically and internationally through a large network of subsidiaries across Africa and in France, with total assets of €40.7 billion (2025).

TAQA Morocco is the first independent power producer in Morocco, operating the largest private coal-fired thermal power plant in the MENA region located in Jorf Lasfar. It supplies a significant share of Morocco's electricity demand and is a subsidiary of the Abu Dhabi-based TAQA Group.

company_name: SGTM (Société Générale des Travaux du Maroc)
description: Société Générale des Travaux du Maroc (SGTM) is one of Morocco's leading construction and civil engineering groups, established in 1971. It delivers large-scale infrastructure, building, and public works projects across sectors including roads, highways, bridges, ports, dams, hydraulic works, industrial facilities, and major buildings. SGTM operates in Morocco and six other African countries and employs over 21,000 people. It was approved for listing on the Casablanca Stock Exchange in November 2025.

LafargeHolcim Maroc S.A. is Morocco's number-one producer and marketer of cement and construction materials. It produces and supplies cement, concrete, aggregates, mortar, and lime, and also engages in treatment and recovery of industrial waste. Its products serve building, industry, energy, port, urban planning, roads, and rail projects. Formerly known as Lafarge Ciments S.A., it was renamed in July 2016 and is a subsidiary of the Holcim Group.

Ciments du Maroc is a Moroccan company that produces cement and ready-made concrete. Its main business activity involves the manufacturing and distribution of various types of cement and concrete products for the construction industry.

TGCC (Travaux Généraux de Construction de Casablanca) is a leading Moroccan construction company founded in 1991, specializing in industrial, public, and building works across residential, hospitality, commercial, industrial, administrative, and infrastructure sectors.

Cosumar is a Moroccan company that produces, packages, and markets sugar products. Its main business activity involves the extraction, refining, packaging, and marketing of sugar, operating as an integrated player in the agri-food sector.

Wafa Assurance SA is a leading Moroccan insurance group and subsidiary of Attijariwafa Bank. Founded in 1972 (as Société Nouvelle d'Assurances), it offers life and non-life insurance products including retirement savings, health, auto, home, and professional insurance. It operates in Morocco and several African countries including Côte d'Ivoire, Senegal, Cameroon, Tunisia, and Egypt.

The Akdital Group is the leading private clinic group in Morocco and the MENA region, operating a network of multidisciplinary and specialized health establishments. Its main business activity is providing a wide range of healthcare services, including cardiology, cardiac surgery, neurosurgery, oncology, and radiotherapy, with a focus on making high-quality health services accessible to a larger portion of the Moroccan population.

TotalEnergies Marketing Maroc is a Moroccan company specializing in the distribution and marketing of oil and gas products across Morocco. It manages a network of service stations and offers a variety of related products and services.

Afriquia Gaz is a Moroccan company specializing in the import, refining, storage, carriage, and distribution of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). Its main business activity is providing energy solutions for domestic and professional markets through an extensive distribution network.

CIH Bank (Crédit Immobilier et Hôtelier) is a Moroccan commercial bank founded in 1920 and headquartered in Casablanca. Originally specialized in real estate and hotel financing, it has evolved into a universal bank offering retail, corporate, and investment banking services with a strong focus on digital transformation. Majority-controlled by CDG Group through Massira Capital Management. In 2024, its consolidated net profit rose 24.3% to MAD 966 million.

Sothema (Société de Thérapeutique Marocaine) is the leading pharmaceutical company in Morocco, founded in 1976, specializing in the manufacturing and marketing of pharmaceutical products including generics, licensed brands, and biotechnologies. It is the first Moroccan company to produce insulin and has pioneered biosimilar production in Africa. It also operates as a CDMO for over 35 global pharmaceutical firms and exports to Europe, Africa, and the Middle East.

Douja Promotion Groupe Addoha S.A. is Morocco's leading real estate developer, founded in 1988. The company develops, manages, and promotes social, medium-income, and luxury residential housing projects across Morocco's major cities including Casablanca, Rabat, Tangier, Meknes, and Oujda.

Crédit du Maroc is a Moroccan commercial bank founded in 1929 (as a Crédit Lyonnais subsidiary). Now headquartered in Casablanca and operating under its current name since 1966, it provides banking, insurance, investment, and securities services to individuals, professionals, and corporates. Since late 2022 it has been a subsidiary of the Holmarcom Group following Crédit Agricole S.A.'s full exit.

Label Vie S.A. is Morocco's leading multi-format retail group, founded in 1985. It operates supermarkets, hypermarkets, and hyper-cash stores under the Carrefour franchise (Carrefour Market, Carrefour, Atacadao) with a network of over 106 stores in 26 cities. It also runs an e-commerce platform (Bringo by Carrefour).

Société Métallurgique d'Imiter (SMI) is a Moroccan mining company established in 1969 and a subsidiary of Managem Group. It specializes in the extraction, exploitation, and marketing of precious and base metals, primarily silver. It operates mines in Morocco and holds assets in Africa through its parent group.
headquarters: Twin Center Tour A, Casablanca, Morocco

description: Lesieur Cristal is a Moroccan agro-industrial company founded in 1941, specializing in edible oils, olive oil, and related food products. It is a pioneer in Morocco's oilseed and olive oil sectors, operating three production sites in Morocco and subsidiaries in Tunisia (Cristal Tunisie) and Senegal (Oleosen). It is majority-controlled by the French group Avril and is listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange.

Alliances Développement Immobilier is a Moroccan real estate development company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It develops and promotes residential, tourism, and commercial real estate projects across Morocco.
Sanlam Maroc is a Moroccan insurance company and subsidiary of the South African Sanlam Group. It provides a range of life and non-life insurance products to individuals and businesses in Morocco.

CFG Bank, formerly known as Casablanca Finance Group, is a Moroccan bank founded in 1992. Initially Morocco's first investment bank, it became a universal bank in 2015, offering retail banking, asset management, and investment services. Listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange in 2023.

AtlantaSanad is a Moroccan insurance company belonging to the Holmarcom Group, offering life, non-life, and investment insurance products. It also holds equity stakes in Moroccan banks including BMCI and CIH Bank as part of its investment portfolio.

company_name: BMCI (Banque Marocaine pour le Commerce et l'Industrie)
description: BMCI (Banque Marocaine pour le Commerce et l'Industrie) is a Moroccan bank founded in 1943 and majority-owned by BNP Paribas. Headquartered in Casablanca, it provides retail and corporate banking services including loans, credit cards, insurance, and investment products.
headquarters: 26 Place des Nations Unies, Casablanca, Morocco

description: Sonasid (Société Nationale de Sidérurgie) is a Moroccan steel manufacturing company founded in 1974, headquartered in Casablanca. It specializes in the production of long steel products (rebar and wire rod) primarily for the construction industry. Sonasid operates industrial plants in Nador and Jorf Lasfar and is jointly controlled by Al Mada and ArcelorMittal through the Nouvelles Sidérurgies Industrielles (NSI) joint venture.

Cash Plus is a Moroccan money transfer and payment services company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It offers domestic and international money transfer, bill payment, and financial services through an extensive agent network across Morocco.
Jet Contractors is a Moroccan construction and civil engineering company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It specializes in building, infrastructure, and public works projects across Morocco.

CMGP Group is a Moroccan company specializing in the distribution of agricultural equipment, irrigation systems, and agri-supplies. It serves farmers and agri-businesses across Morocco and is listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange.

Compagnie Minière de Touissit (CMT) is a Moroccan mining company founded in 1974, engaged in the exploration, mining, and processing of base metals, precious metals, and industrial minerals. It primarily extracts lead and zinc ores and operates underground mines at Tighza and Rehamna in Morocco.
Société des Boissons du Maroc (SBM) is a Moroccan beverages company controlled by the Castel Group. It produces, markets, and distributes beer, soft drinks, and water across Morocco.

Aradei Capital is a Moroccan real estate investment company (REIT) affiliated with the CFG Group. It develops and manages commercial real estate assets including shopping centers, retail parks, and logistics platforms across Morocco.

Delta Holding is a Moroccan industrial group listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It operates across multiple industrial segments including technical textile and protective equipment, environment and maintenance services, signage and road safety equipment, and construction materials.

Risma is Morocco's leading hotel operator, managing a portfolio of hotels primarily under the Accor Group franchise brands (Ibis, Novotel, Mercure, etc.). It is listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange and serves the tourism and hospitality sector across Morocco.

Auto Nejma is a Moroccan automotive distributor listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It holds the exclusive distribution rights for Mercedes-Benz vehicles in Morocco and offers related after-sales services.

Auto Hall is a Moroccan vehicle distribution group listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It distributes a range of automotive brands across Morocco and provides related financial and after-sales services.
Vicenne is a Moroccan healthcare company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange, specializing in optical and eyecare retail services through a network of optician stores in Morocco.

Hightech Payment Systems (HPS) is a Moroccan fintech multinational founded in 1995, specializing in electronic payment software solutions and services. Its flagship product, PowerCARD, serves over 500 institutions in 95 countries. HPS operates through subsidiaries across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia. In 2024, it reported revenues of MAD 1.267 billion ($140 million), a 6.4% year-on-year increase.
headquarters: Casablanca Nearshore Park, 1100 Boulevard Al Qods, Casablanca, Morocco

description: Résidences Dar Saada is a Moroccan real estate developer listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange, affiliated with the Palmeraie Group. It focuses on the development and promotion of affordable and mid-range residential housing across Morocco.

Eaux Minérales d'Oulmès is a Moroccan beverage company controlled by the Holmarcom Group. It produces and markets mineral and sparkling water under the Oulmès, Ain Ifrane, and Sidi Ali brands, and is one of the leading mineral water producers in Morocco.
Eqdom is a Moroccan consumer finance company and subsidiary of Société Générale. It provides personal loans, auto loans, and revolving credit to individuals and professionals across Morocco.
AFMA is a Moroccan insurance brokerage company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides insurance intermediation and risk management services to corporate and individual clients.
Afric Industries is a Moroccan industrial manufacturing company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange, producing industrial equipment and components for various sectors.

AGMA is a Moroccan insurance and reinsurance brokerage company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides risk consulting and insurance intermediation services to large corporations and institutions.

Aluminium du Maroc is a Moroccan company specializing in the manufacturing and distribution of aluminium profiles and systems for the construction industry. It is independently listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange.
Balima is a Moroccan insurance company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange and affiliated with CDG Group. It primarily offers life insurance and pension savings products.

Cartier Saada is a Moroccan agri-food company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It operates in the olive oil and canned olive sector, producing and marketing olive products for domestic and export markets.
Invictus Investments is a Moroccan investment holding company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange, focused on diversified financial and real estate investments.

M2M Group is a Moroccan fintech and IT company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides digital payment solutions, electronic ID systems, and smart card technologies to banks, telecom operators, and government institutions.

Microdata is a Moroccan IT services company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides technology solutions including software, systems integration, and IT infrastructure services to businesses and public entities in Morocco.
Rebab Company is a Moroccan real estate company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange, focused on the development and management of commercial and office real estate assets.

SNEP (Société Nationale d'Électrolyse et de Pétrochimie) is a Moroccan chemicals company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It produces PVC resin and chlorine-soda products through electrolysis, primarily serving the construction and industry sectors. OCP Group holds a partial stake.
SNP is a Moroccan petrochemicals company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It produces polyethylene and other petrochemical products for industrial and packaging applications.

Stroc Industrie is a Moroccan industrial engineering and maintenance company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides industrial construction, maintenance, and project management services to energy, mining, and manufacturing clients.

Stokvis Nord Afrique is a Moroccan industrial distribution company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It distributes industrial equipment, tools, and automotive supplies to businesses in Morocco.
Timar is a Moroccan logistics and freight forwarding company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides customs brokerage, freight management, warehousing, and supply chain services.

Unimer is a Moroccan agro-industrial company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It processes and exports seafood and agricultural products, particularly canned sardines, fishmeal, and fish oil, primarily for international markets.

Disway (formerly Distrisoft) is a Moroccan IT products distribution company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It distributes computer hardware, software, networking equipment, and IT peripherals to resellers and enterprises in Morocco.

Colorado is a Moroccan paint and coatings company founded in 1962 and majority-owned by the Berrada family. It specializes in the manufacture and distribution of building paints, industrial paints, automotive refinishing paints, and functional coatings under brands including Stella, Rovana, Ambra, and Touareg.

IB Maroc (IB Maroc.com SA) is a Moroccan IT services and digital solutions company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides software development, systems integration, and digital transformation services to Moroccan businesses.

Immorente Invest is a Moroccan real estate investment company (OPCI) listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It invests in and manages a diversified portfolio of commercial and office real estate assets across Morocco.

Maghreb Oxygène is a Moroccan industrial gases company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It produces and distributes industrial, medical, and specialty gases including oxygen, nitrogen, argon, and acetylene, and is partially affiliated with the Air Liquide Group.
Med Paper is a Moroccan paper and packaging company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It produces kraft paper, packaging materials, and cardboard products for industrial and commercial use.

Nexans Maroc is the Moroccan subsidiary of the French Nexans Group, listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It manufactures and distributes energy cables, telecom cables, and wiring systems for construction, energy, and industrial applications.

S2M is a Moroccan payment technology company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It develops and markets electronic payment software and card management solutions for banks and financial institutions in Morocco and Africa.
Salafin is a Moroccan consumer finance company and subsidiary of Bank of Africa Group. It provides personal loans, auto financing, and revolving credit products to individuals in Morocco.

Sofac is a Moroccan consumer finance company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange, partially affiliated with Crédit Agricole. It specializes in personal and auto loans for individuals and professionals.

Snecma Maroc is a Moroccan aerospace maintenance and services company and subsidiary of the Safran Group. It provides maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) services for aircraft engines and aerospace components.

Société de Réalisations Mécaniques (SRM) is a Moroccan industrial engineering company listed on the Casablanca Stock Exchange. It provides mechanical engineering, fabrication, and maintenance services for industrial clients.
